qt: Lysine is represented by one-letter code i.e.,
op: I
Y
L
K
ca: K

qt: At isoelectric pH, an amino acid exists as
op: None of these
Cation
Zwitterion
Anion
ca: Zwitterion

qt: Which of the following is not the function of proteins?
op: Fight against the invading pathogens
Helps in transporting oxygen in the blood
Helps in digesting food
Carries genetic information
ca: Carries genetic information

qt: The peptide sequence tags are ……………............
op: The partial sequence of a peptide that can be revealed by consecutive peaks
The sequence of a peptide that is produced after MS2
The variation in the fragmentation site of a peptide
The mass difference between consecutive peaks in a peptide sequence
ca: The partial sequence of a peptide that can be revealed by consecutive peaks

qt: Which of the following statements is un-true about 1’ (Primary) structure of proteins?
op: The 1-D (Primary) structure of proteins can be determined by Edman Degradation
The sequence of amino acids joined by a peptide bond
The Primary OR 1’ structure of a protein determines its initial properties.
The 1-D (Primary) structure of proteins can be determined by Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR)
ca: The 1-D (Primary) structure of proteins can be determined by Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR)

qt: Which of the following statements are true about 3-D (Tertiary) structure of proteins?
op: Primary structure of proteins determines the tertiary structure
3’ Structures of proteins are formed as a result of hydrophobic interactions between polar R-groups
Combinations of Alpha helices, Beta sheets, coils and loops help form 3’ structures
3’ structures are formed by different peptide chains that make up the proteins
ca: Combinations of Alpha helices, Beta sheets, coils and loops help form 3’ structures

qt: Which of the following is responsible for determining a protein’s 3D shape?
op: Peptide bond
Interaction with molecular chaperons
Interaction with other polypeptides
Amino acid sequence
ca: Amino acid sequence

qt: The significance of the number of PSTs that a protein reports ……..............
op: The more PSTs a protein reports, the more likely it is to be a peptide
The more PSTs a protein reports, the more likely it is to be a protein fragment
All of the these
The more PSTs a protein reports, the more likely it is to be the precursor protein
ca: The more PSTs a protein reports, the more likely it is to be the precursor protein

qt: Beeta-pleated sheets are the examples of ...........................
op: 2’ OR 2-D OR Secondary Structure of proteins
4’ OR 4-D OR Quaternary Structure of proteins
1’ OR 1-D OR Primary Structure of proteins
3’ OR 3-D OR Tertiary Structure of proteins
ca: 2’ OR 2-D OR Secondary Structure of proteins

qt: The factors that affect the mass difference between consecutive peaks in a peptide sequence ……….......................
op: The mass of the amino acids
All of the above
The fragmentation site of the peptide
The type of fragmentation technique
ca: The mass of the amino acids

qt: The purpose of MS2 in protein identification is ………..................
op: To identify the protein in the sample
To determine the sequence of the protein
To identify the fragmentation technique used
To measure the mass of the intact molecule in sample
ca: To determine the sequence of the protein

qt: Which of the following statements is un-true about 2’ (Secondary) structure of proteins?
op: 2’ Structures of proteins are formed as a result of Hydrogen bond formation between N and C termini in a protein backbone
2’ structures of proteins include Alpha helices and Beta sheets
2’ Structures of proteins are formed as a result of peptides bond formation between N and C termini in a protein backbone
Hydrogen bonds are the reason of 2’ structures of proteins
ca: 2’ Structures of proteins are formed as a result of peptides bond formation between N and C termini in a protein backbone

qt: Which of the following statements is un-true about proteins?
op: Proteins properties are linked to the properties of amino acids.
Proteins structures are organized into 1’, 2’, 3’, 4’ modular conformations
Proteins are made by transcription of amino acids
Proteins spontaneously fold to take 3D forms
ca: Proteins are made by transcription of amino acids

qt: How many bonded atoms are required to constitute a dihedral angle, such as phi or psi?
Select the appropriate option of fragmentation techniques that can be used in MS2?
op: CID
ECD
ETD
All of the above
ca: All of the above

qt: Proteins are made-up of amino acids; these amino acids are linked together by which of the following bonds?
op: Peptide bond
Ionic bond
Hydrogen bond
Disulfide bond
ca: Peptide bond

qt: The final resort in protein search and identification …………...............
op: Comparing the mass of the intact molecule in the sample with the mass of known proteins
Determining the sequence of the protein
Identifying the fragmentation technique used
Matching experimental fragments with in silico fragments
ca: Matching experimental fragments with in silico fragments

qt: The significance of the score that is calculated when comparing the mass of the intact molecule from MS2 with the theoretical mass of database proteins ……………...............
op: The score is a measure of the confidence that the protein that was originally analyzed has been identified
The score is a measure of the accuracy of the mass spectrometer
The score is a measure of the fragmentation efficiency of MS2
The score is a measure of the molecular weight of the protein
ca: The score is a measure of the confidence that the protein that was originally analyzed has been identified

qt: The peptide sequence tags used to identify proteins ………….............
op: By comparing the mass difference between consecutive peaks to the mass of known amino acids
By using a database of known peptide sequence tags to identify the protein
All of the above
By comparing the mass of the peptide sequence tag to the mass of known proteins
ca: By using a database of known peptide sequence tags to identify the protein

qt: Which one of the following bonds, is the reason of 2-D (2’ OR Secondary) structure formation of protein?
op: Peptides bond
Hydrogen bond
Ionic bond
Covalent bond
ca: Hydrogen bond

qt: Which of the following statements is true about proteins?
op: Proteins are polymers of amino acids
Proteins are polymers of glucose
Proteins are polymers of peptide bonds
Proteins are polymers of di-sulfide bridges
ca: Proteins are polymers of amino acids

qt: The purpose of MS1 in tandem mass spectrometry (MS/MS) is …………..............
op: To fragment the intact sample molecule into two parts
To measure the molecular weight of the intake sample molecule
To match the experimental mass with theoretical peptides from a database
To measure the mass of the fragments produced by MS2
ca: To measure the molecular weight of the intake sample molecule

qt: The 1-D (Primary) structure of proteins can be determined by ...............................
op: Tandem Mass Spectrometry
Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR)
Both Edman Degradation & NMR
Edman Degradation
ca: Edman Degradation

qt: The purpose of MS1 in protein identification ……………...........
op: To identify the fragmentation technique used
To measure the mass of the intact molecule in the sample
To determine the sequence of the protein
To identify the protein in the sample
ca: To measure the mass of the intact molecule in the sample

qt: How is the experimental mass reported from MS2 matched with theoretical peptides from a database?
op: The experimental mass is matched to the theoretical mass of the peptide with the closest match
The experimental mass is used to calculate the sequence of the peptide
Both experimental mass is compared to the theoretical masses of all the peptides in the database & experimental mass is matched to the theoretical mass of the peptide with the closest match
The experimental mass is compared to the theoretical masses of all the peptides in the database
ca: Both experimental mass is compared to the theoretical masses of all the peptides in the database & experimental mass is matched to the theoretical mass of the peptide with the closest match

qt: Which of the following statements are true about quaternary structure of proteins?
op: 4’ (Quaternary) structure of proteins are formed by peptide chains that make-up the protein
4’ Structures of proteins are formed as a result of hydrophobic interactions between non-polar R-groups
Combinations of Alpha helices, Beta sheets, coils and loops help form 3’ structures
Proteins comprised of only single chain have a quaternary structure
ca: 4’ (Quaternary) structure of proteins are formed by peptide chains that make-up the protein
